title: Address reuse when set_diversifier_index write fails after Sapling skip
description: Trigger: set_diversifier_index(0, found_u128+1) fails (non-fatal warn) AND Sapling skipped invalid diversifiers (found_di > start). Result: DB stays at start+1. Next next_diversifier() returns start+1; find_address(start+1) finds the same found_di again; identical UA is sent to second payer. Reuse continues until DB write succeeds or process restarts. The condition found_u128+1 > start+1 is always true when skipping occurred, so every skip event is vulnerable if the subsequent set_diversifier_index fails. In practice SQLite writes rarely fail, but under DB pressure or filesystem issues this silently breaks payment address unlinkability — the core privacy guarantee.
